changes.text: Invitation import preview — each row is now a collapsible card instead of a wide table. Much easier to scan and edit, and error rows auto-expand so you land on exactly what needs attention.
Bulk invitation import — upload an Excel sheet to create dozens of invitations at once. Download the included template from the Import page to share with your partner while building the guest list.
Structured mailing address — each invitation now has separate fields for Address Line 1, Line 2, City, State, and Postal Code. Ready to drive printable mailing labels later.
RSVP form mailing address is now 5 separate inputs that pre-fill from whatever the admin imported. Saves the same structured data back on submit.

RSVP confirmation emails — guests get a themed email after submitting or editing their RSVP, with a recap of what they told you and a link to change it before the deadline. Turn on at Features → Email features.
Day-of broadcasts — send timely email updates (ceremony timing, shuttle delays, weather pivots) to guests who opted in. Compose from Broadcasts and see delivery status per household.
Email heading and footer overrides — customize the text at the top and bottom of every email you send. Configured at Email Settings.
Free-text RSVP questions — add a new 'Free-text (large box)' question type for anything that doesn't fit a fixed list of choices.

Seating chart — no more typing guest names. Click an unseated guest to add them to the selected table, or 'Seat whole household' in one click. Seated guests get Move-to and Remove buttons for quick reshuffling.
Admin RSVP edit modal — now mirrors the public RSVP form exactly. You can edit attending guests, plus-ones, meals, address, contact email, and custom RSVP questions from one place.
RSVP export — the CSV now has one row per attendee (instead of one row per invitation), with per-person meal info, so caterers and printers see the full roster.
RSVP Option editor — the per-choice Description field now only shows when it's actually used (meal options).
Dashboard now counts plus-ones in Total Guests and Meal Choices.
Editing an RSVP from the admin side no longer silently wipes the guest's plus-ones.
Confirmation emails render attending guest names instead of their internal IDs.
Confirmation email greetings drop the leading 'The' (so 'The Johnson Family' reads as 'Hi Johnson Family,').
Drag-to-reorder within wedding-party groups — rearrange bridesmaids / groomsmen / other role groups by dragging, without retyping the order numbers.
